title: podalyria lupinoides
intro: Podalyria lupinoides, also known as the Sweet Thorn or Wolf's Milk, is a small shrub native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. In traditional herbal medicine, Podalyria lupinoides is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be used topically as a poultice or applied directly to affected areas.
